role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; one of three subjects of a joint oral history with Toni Breaux and Willie Elliot Jenkins (catalog entry 46).
ai_reading: Geraldine Crawford Bennett shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 46 (catalog slug 'geraldine-crawford-bennett-toni-breaux-and-willie-elliot-jenkins'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Geraldine Crawford Bennett's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript. Geraldine Crawford Bennett is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 46 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Geraldine Crawford Bennett; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 46
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Robert Bob Hicks House, Bogalusa, Louisiana
gallery.caption: The Robert Bob Hicks House in Bogalusa, Louisiana, base of the Bogalusa Civic and Voters League and the Deacons for Defense and Justice that protected the Jenkins family during the local movement Geraldine Crawford Bennett describes.
